mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS is increasing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) captures a broad-based expansion in both supply and final demand, which boosts corporate revenue visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined directional signal from INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) during late-cycle expansions signal a contraction in residential investment that drags aggregate demand and credit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, negative HOUST changes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 1.5% over three months signals accelerating inflation that pressures corporate margins and prompts tighter Federal Reserve policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where CPIAUCSL rises more than 1.5% in three months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income growth and consumer confidence, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered instances where UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in three months are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated FEDFUNDS and low UNRATE signal a restrictive real policy stance that tightens financial conditions and suppresses equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint level of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms durable aggregate demand expansion that supports top-line revenue growth across the economy, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
